title: helianthus nuttallii subsp. parishii
intro: Helianthus nuttallii subsp. parishii, also known as Parish's sunflower or desert sunflower, is a rare and unique species of the sunflower family, characterized by its tall stature and vibrant yellow flowers. Traditionally, the plant has been used in herbal medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, making it useful in treating various health conditions. The health benefits of Helianthus nuttallii subsp. parishii include reducing inflammation, soothing digestive issues, and supporting immune function. The plant can be prepared as a herbal tea, tincture, or infusion, and its root is sometimes used in traditional medicine to create topical creams and salves for skin conditions.
